checks.title: Regression: Complete BullMQ + Redis job queue system end-to-end
checks.notes: Verified at fix time - Full system working: bullmq/ioredis installed, 4 queues (email-scan/unsubscribe/bulk-delete/rollup-digest) configured with retry/backoff, Redis connection via REDIS_URL, workers initialized via instrumentation.ts at project root, all API routes use queue.add() instead of direct calls, scheduled rollup digest with '0 8 * * *' pattern, cleanup on preference removal, README.md documents Redis requirement

checks.title: Regression: Instrumentation file at project root for Next.js to load
checks.notes: Verified at fix time - instrumentation.ts is at project root (not src/), Next.js requires this location to find and execute the register() function on startup

checks.title: Regression: BullMQ worker lifecycle management via instrumentation
checks.notes: Verified at fix time - instrumentation.ts initializes workers on startup and closes them on beforeExit/SIGINT/SIGTERM. Removed auto-initialization from workers.ts to prevent duplicate workers in dev hot-reload

checks.title: Regression: Daily rollup digest scheduling with cleanup
checks.notes: Verified at fix time - scheduleDailyRollup creates repeatable job with pattern '0 8 * * *', removeScheduledRollup is now called when last rollup preference is removed (in both /action and /bulk routes)

checks.title: Regression: BullMQ + Redis job queue system implemented
checks.notes: Verified at fix time - bullmq and ioredis packages installed, queues created for email-scan/unsubscribe/bulk-delete/rollup-digest, API routes updated to use queue.add() instead of direct function calls, docker-compose.yml includes Redis service, .env.example has REDIS_URL configuration

checks.title: Regression: Library modules source correctly in bash/zsh
checks.notes: Verified at fix time - V3 smoke check passes for all lib/*.sh modules in both bash and zsh. Used ${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-$0} for zsh compatibility, moved LAZYDAVE_DIR setup before source guard

checks.title: Health check endpoint returns database connectivity status
checks.steps: Run `curl localhost:3000/api/health` and verify response contains `{status: 'healthy', database: 'connected'}` when database is available

checks.title: Gmail webhook endpoint verifies HMAC SHA256 signature
checks.steps: Send POST request to `/api/webhook/gmail` with invalid signature and verify it returns 401 Unauthorized; send with valid signature derived from GMAIL_WEBHOOK_SECRET and verify it accepts the request

checks.title: Subscription subjects endpoint returns recent email subjects by sender
checks.steps: Run `curl '/api/subscriptions/subjects?senderEmail=test@example.com'` with authenticated session and verify response contains `subjects` array with up to 15 email subject lines

checks.title: Scan-sender endpoint rescans subscriptions after bulk delete
checks.steps: After performing bulk delete on a sender, send POST to `/api/subscriptions/scan-sender` with `{senderEmail: '...'}` and verify subscription is updated with new message count or removed if zero messages remain

checks.title: Sync reset endpoint clears all user subscription data
checks.steps: Send POST to `/api/sync/reset` with authenticated session and verify response contains `success: true` and `deletedCount` reflecting number of subscriptions deleted; confirm subsequent scan starts as full scan

checks.title: Scan status endpoint returns real-time scan progress
checks.steps: After triggering a scan, poll `/api/scan/status` and verify response contains `scanStatus`, `scanProgress`, `scanTotal` fields that update during scanning

checks.title: Landing page displays privacy-first messaging and feature cards
checks.steps: Navigate to `/` and verify page contains: privacy badge shield icon, main headline 'Take Back Control of Your Inbox', three feature cards (Bulk Deletion, Smart Grouping, True Unsubscribe), 'Better than Unroll.me' comparison section

checks.title: How-it-works page displays 4-step process and detection explanation
checks.steps: Navigate to `/how-it-works` and verify: 4 step cards with icons (Connect, Scan, Choose, Clean), 'How We Detect Email Groups' section with high/medium confidence signals, True Unsubscribe vs Filtering comparison cards

checks.title: Rollup page displays email groups in digest with delivery schedule
checks.steps: Navigate to `/dashboard/rollup` and verify: list of subscriptions with rollup action, delivery time display (default 08:00), frequency set to 'Daily', 'Send Test Digest' and 'Disable Rollup' buttons

checks.title: Settings page displays three tabs: Account, Notifications, Privacy
checks.steps: Navigate to `/dashboard/settings` and verify: tab sidebar with Account/Notifications/Privacy options, Account tab shows read-only name/email from Google, Privacy tab has disabled 'Delete Everything' button

checks.title: Deploy page displays Docker Compose and configuration instructions
checks.steps: Navigate to `/deploy` and verify: three deployment option cards (Docker Compose recommended, Pre-built image, Build from source), required environment variables section (DATABASE_URL, NEXTAUTH_URL, NEXTAUTH_SECRET, ENCRYPTION_KEY), Gmail scopes warning card

checks.title: OAuth tokens are encrypted at rest in database using AES-256
checks.steps: Connect a Google account, then query database `Account` table directly and verify `access_token` and `refresh_token` columns contain encrypted strings (not plaintext); verify `lib/crypto.ts` implements AES-256-GCM encryption

checks.title: Prisma middleware automatically encrypts/decrypts tokens on read/write
checks.steps: Review `src/lib/db.ts` and verify encryption middleware extends Prisma client with `create`/`update` hooks that call `encrypt()` and `find*` hooks that call `decrypt()` for `account` model tokens

checks.title: Unsubscription attempts are tracked with status and error messages
checks.steps: After attempting unsubscribe, query `UnsubscriptionAttempt` table and verify records contain: `userId`, `subscriptionId`, `method`, `status` (pending/success/failed), `errorMessage` (if failed), `attemptedAt` timestamp

checks.title: Bulk deletion jobs track total and deleted message counts
checks.notes: Verified at fix time - runBulkDelete now creates BulkDeletionJob records with userId, senderEmail, status (pending/success/failed), totalMessages, deletedMessages, completedAt timestamp. Follows same pattern as runUnsubscribe for UnsubscriptionAttempt records.

checks.title: Subscription list supports sort by email count, date, name, confidence
checks.steps: On dashboard, open sort dropdown and verify options: Most/Least Recent, Most/Least Emails, Name (A-Z/Z-A), Highest/Lowest Confidence; select each option and verify list reorders correctly

checks.title: Subscription list filter buttons work correctly (all, high-confidence, low-confidence, has-unsubscribe)
checks.steps: On dashboard, click each filter button and verify: 'All' shows all subscriptions with count, 'High Confidence' shows only subscriptions with confidence >= 70%, 'Low Confidence' shows < 50%, 'Has Unsubscribe' shows only those with listUnsubscribeHeader

checks.title: Subscription list bulk actions (delete all, unsubscribe) process selected items
checks.steps: Select multiple subscriptions using checkboxes, verify 'N selected' badge appears, click 'Delete All Emails' and confirm deletion dialog shows correct email counts; click 'Unsubscribe' and verify all selected are queued for unsubscription

checks.title: Subscription card expandable section shows recent email subjects
checks.steps: Click chevron on any subscription card and verify expandable section appears showing 'Recent emails from this sender:' with up to 15 subject lines; verify loading spinner appears while fetching via `/api/subscriptions/subjects`

checks.title: Subscription list height is resizable with drag handle and persists to localStorage
checks.steps: Drag the resize handle at bottom of subscription list and verify height changes; double-click handle to reset to default; reload page and verify saved height persists from localStorage key 'subscription-list-height'

checks.title: Scan button displays status (idle, scanning, error, success) with icon changes
checks.steps: On dashboard, observe Scan button in idle state shows 'Scan Inbox'; click to start scan and verify button shows 'Scanning...' with spinner and progress percentage; after completion, verify success state or error message if failed

checks.title: Theme toggle switches between light and dark modes
checks.steps: Click theme toggle button in header and verify: page switches between light/dark color schemes; theme persists across page reloads using localStorage or user preference

checks.title: Regression: BullMQ queues lazy-loaded to prevent build-time Redis connections
checks.notes: Verified at fix time - Queues and Redis connection are now lazy-loaded via getter functions (getRedisConnection, getEmailScanQueue, etc.) to prevent connection attempts during Next.js build. Build completes successfully without Redis errors even when Redis is not running.

checks.title: Regression: BullMQ + Redis job queue system fully implemented per spec
checks.notes: Verified at fix time - BullMQ (v5.74.1) and ioredis (v5.10.1) packages installed, 4 queues (email-scan/unsubscribe/bulk-delete/rollup-digest) configured with retry logic (attempts: 3, exponential backoff), workers initialized via instrumentation.ts with proper lifecycle management, scheduled daily rollup digest with '0 8 * * *' pattern, all API routes use queue.add() instead of direct async calls, Redis connection via REDIS_URL env var, docker-compose.yml includes Redis service
